The '''''Myrkos''''' was a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Dreadnaught/Legends|dreadnaught]] [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] in service during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. It was stationed in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] around [[Tembora]], a mobile [[rogue moon]] that housed the Republic's [[Tembora Research Station]]. At one point, enemy forces launched an [[Attack on the Tembora Research Station|attack]] on the research station, with the ''Myrkos'' becoming critically damaged during the fighting. The [[Starship/Legends|starship]] crashed into the rogue [[Moon/Legends|moon]], leaving behind a [[Radiation/Legends|radioactive]] crater and altering the mobile celestial body's course in [[Realspace/Legends|space]].

==History==
[[File:Tembora-Challenge61.png|thumb|right|300px|During the Clone Wars, the ''Myrkos'' crashed into the rogue moon Tembora.]]
The ''Myrkos'' was a [[Dreadnaught/Legends|dreadnaught]] [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] that was in service of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]],<ref name="Challenge61" /> which pitted the Republic against the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> and were fought between [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] and [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]].<ref name="Dates" /> It was stationed in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] around [[Tembora]], a mobile [[rogue moon]] with a diameter of 1,200 [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] that housed the [[Tembora Research Station]], where the Republic's [[Army Weapons Research Department]] was developing the [[KLR series combat droid]]. The Republic took precautions that only the [[Commanding officer/Legends|command]] and [[Republic officer/Legends|officers]] of the ''Myrkos'' were aware of Tembora's location.<ref name="Challenge61" />

At one point during the Clone Wars, enemy forces<ref name="Challenge61" /> affiliated with the Confederacy of Independent Systems<ref name="NEC" /> nevertheless discovered the Tembora Research Station and launched an [[Attack on the Tembora Research Station|attack]] on it. During the ensuing engagement, the ''Myrkos'' became severely damaged and crashed into the rogue [[Moon/Legends|moon]] thirty kilometers away from the research station. The [[Starship/Legends|starship]] was destroyed, leaving a two-kilometer-wide crater on Tembora's surface that had high residual [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]] levels and had debris from the vessel scattered for kilometers around it. The impact of the ''Myrkos'' on Tembora caused a slight alteration in the moon's course, and the celestial body became lost in [[Realspace/Legends|space]].<ref name="Challenge61" />

{{RPG|"[[Rogue Metal]]"}}
[[Standard decade/Legends|Decades]] later, at one point during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], a group of [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] crash-landed on Tembora after its trajectory had intersected one of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]]. During the group's investigation of the Tembora Research Station, the spacers discovered the site of the ''Myrkos''{{'s}} crash via perusing the [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] information at the station's command center.<ref name="Challenge61" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Myrkos'' was introduced in the [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure "[[Rogue Metal]]," which was authored by [[James B. King]] and published in the [[June]] [[1992]] [[Challenge 61|sixty-first issue]] of the [[Challenge (magazine)|''Challenge'']] magazine for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Challenge61" /> The adventure is considered [[Canon|noncanonical]] with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="Unlicensed">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> The adventure suggests that the player characters can investigate the crash site of the starship, where they find debris from the vessel. This article assumes the scenario plays out as described.<ref name="Challenge61" />
